title: Lighthouse Finality
description: The finality health of the archive pair's consensus client. Upstream lighthouse-metrics has no finality board - teku's does - so this fills that gap, mirroring teku's Trailing Distance and Participation panels. Finalized is the irreversible tip: an epoch justified two epochs running, unwindable only by burning a third of the total stake. Watch finalisation delay (head minus finalized) and participation staying above two-thirds.

panels.title: finalized epoch
panels.description: The irreversible tip. Should climb steadily, trailing head by ~2 epochs.

panels.title: justified epoch
panels.description: One step short of finalized: >2/3 of stake voted for this epoch.

panels.title: finalisation delay (epochs behind head)
panels.description: Head epoch minus finalized epoch. ~2 is healthy. Climbing above ~4 and staying there is finality stalling - the single most serious consensus failure.

panels.title: participation (prev epoch)
panels.description: Share of active stake that attested to the correct target. Must stay above 2/3 (66.7%) for the chain to finalize at all.

panels.title: head / justified / finalized epoch
panels.description: The three-step ladder to irreversibility. Healthy: three lines climbing together, finalized trailing head by ~2. Finality stalling shows as finalized flattening while head keeps climbing - the gap between the top and bottom lines widening.

panels.title: finalisation delay
panels.description: The gap above, plotted. A flat low line is health; a rising line is the alarm.

panels.title: Participation (the root cause when finality stalls)
participation rate vs the 2/3 finality threshold
panels.description: Finality needs >2/3 of stake attesting correctly. If this line falls below the marked threshold, the chain cannot finalize - this is what to check first when finalisation delay climbs.
